mnemonics: FLARe: Forward Pass, Loss Calculation, Backward Pass (Backpropagation), Update Rule (e.g., Gradient Descent)
keyPoints: Supervised learning uses labeled data (input-output pairs) for training.
Neural networks learn hierarchical representations through layers of neurons.
Backpropagation efficiently calculates the gradient of the loss function with respect to all weights.
commonMistakes: Using too deep a network for a simple problem, leading to overfitting.
Forgetting to normalize or preprocess input data, causing slow or unstable training.
